nodes.name: Claim Next Batch (API)
nodes.notes: Replaces the old Reap Stale Processing Items / Count In-Progress Items / Check Capacity / Claim Next Batch Postgres+Code+If nodes -- ingest-worker's /ai-queue/claim now does all four steps in one call (reap stale, count in-progress, compute capacity, atomically claim up to parallel_limit crop_status='done' rows FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED). Tune throughput by editing the query params below. Returns {events: [...]} -- empty if no capacity or no work.

nodes.name: Split Claimed Events
nodes.notes: Splits the {events: [...]} array from Claim Next Batch (API) into one n8n item per claimed event -- an HTTP node's raw JSON response doesn't reliably auto-split into items across n8n versions, so this is explicit rather than relied-upon.

nodes.name: Is Person?
nodes.notes: Routes each claimed row by its object type -- person goes to the person-description prompt/table, everything else (car/truck, guaranteed by Claim Next Batch (API)'s object_types param) goes to the vehicle-attributes+plate prompt/table.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "messages": [ { "role": "user", "content": [ { "type": "text", "text": "Describe this person's clothing colors and what they appear to be doing, in one short sentence. No speculation about identity." }, { "type": "image_url", "image_url": { "url": "data:image/jpeg;base64," + $json.crop_image_base64 } } ] } ], "temperature": 0 } }}

nodes.name: Insert Person Sighting (API)
nodes.notes: Replaces the old Insert Person Sighting + Mark Done Postgres node pair -- ingest-worker's POST /sightings/persons inserts the sighting and marks ai_status='done' in one DB transaction.

nodes.name: Call Qwen (Attributes + Plate)
nodes.notes: Merged attributes + plate OCR into one call for speed (one ~45-90s LLM round trip per vehicle event instead of two). Trade-off: Qwen isn't a dedicated OCR specialist, so plate_text_llm may be less accurate than a separate-OCR-model approach -- plate_text_frigate (Frigate's own LPR read) is kept alongside it as a cross-check either way.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "messages": [ { "role": "user", "content": [ { "type": "text", "text": "Look at this cropped photo of a vehicle. Respond with ONLY a JSON object, no other text before or after it, no markdown, no explanation: {\"color\": string, \"body_type\": string (e.g. sedan/suv/truck/van/pickup/minivan), \"make\": string or null if unsure, \"make_confidence\": \"low\"|\"medium\"|\"high\", \"model\": string or null if unsure (e.g. \"F-150\", \"Camry\", \"Model 3\" -- the specific model, not just the make), \"model_confidence\": \"low\"|\"medium\"|\"high\", \"notable_features\": string or null, a short phrase (under 10 words) for anything visually distinctive -- roof rack, ladder rack, trailer hitch, bike rack, tinted windows, decals or commercial branding/text, visible damage or rust, unusual color/wrap, aftermarket wheels/lift kit -- use null if the vehicle looks unremarkable, \"plate_text\": string or null}. plate_text must be ONLY the plate's characters (e.g. \"10MG407\") with no spaces, punctuation, labels, or reasoning -- if no plate is visible or legible, use null. Do not describe your process." }, { "type": "image_url", "image_url": { "url": "data:image/jpeg;base64," + $json.crop_image_base64 } } ] } ], "temperature": 0 } }}

nodes.name: Insert Vehicle Sighting (API)
nodes.notes: Replaces the old Insert Vehicle Sighting + Mark Done Postgres node pair -- ingest-worker's POST /sightings/vehicles inserts the sighting and marks ai_status='done' in one DB transaction.

nodes.name: Handle Failure (API)
nodes.notes: Any VLM call's error branch (person or vehicle path) lands here. ingest-worker's POST /ai-queue/{id}/fail runs the same retry-or-fail-with-cap logic the old Handle Failure (Retry or Fail) Postgres node did: below max_attempts goes back to ai_status='retry' (picked up again on a future run, not looped in this execution), at/above it becomes 'failed' (terminal).
